user_message: Just got this from Sarah Wilson at Litware Inc -- I need help figuring out deployment blockers. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 17:48 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Litware Inc

Our team encountered some challenges with deployment blockers. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 10:40 EST
Subject: Re: deployment blockers Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 14:30 EST
Subject: deployment blockers - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for deployment blockers. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Litware Inc: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-I trap (MSX hygiene catchup proposals don't acknowledge dark-period staleness); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-I failure mode (MSX hygiene catchup proposals don't acknowledge dark-period staleness)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-I -- MSX hygiene catchup proposals don't acknowledge dark-period staleness
